frameworks.name: activity flow modeling
frameworks.one_line: simulate neural activity propagating over empirically-estimated functional connectivity

frameworks.name: conjunction hubs
frameworks.one_line: brain regions selectively integrating sensory, cognitive, and motor activations

frameworks.name: empirically-constrained neural network model
frameworks.one_line: task-performing network built from fMRI functional connectivity during cognitive control

frameworks.name: dynamic cognitive information transformation
frameworks.one_line: adaptive task behavior arising from flexible transformation of cognitive representations

core_question: Can empirically-estimated functional connectivity networks simulate task performance and reveal how conjunction hubs integrate information for adaptive behavior?
key_assumptions: functional connectivity maps the flow of activity between brain regions
conjunction hubs selectively integrate sensory, cognitive, and motor signals
adaptive task behavior emerges from dynamic transformations of cognitive information
manipulations: cognitive control task with sensory and task rule inputs
simulated activity flow over an empirically-estimated connectivity model
comparison of simulated motor outputs against chance task performance
key_findings: empirically-specified simulations produced above-chance task performance
simulations integrated sensory and task rule activations within conjunction hubs
task-performing model could be constructed directly from fMRI connectivity data
interpretations: conjunction hubs support flexible cognitive computations via representational integration
empirically-estimated neural network models are a feasible tool for studying human cognitive computations
